title: NIS2 Incident Reporting Timeline Compliance (24h Early Warning / 72h Notification)

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIS2 Art.23 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.23 (Reporting obligations) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.33: NIS2 Incident Reporting Timeline Compliance (24h Early Warning / 72h Notification).
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.33' running on notable macro, index=itsm (regulatory filing tasks with custom fields), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `` `notable` `` macro, `index=itsm` (regulatory filing tasks with custom fields)
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928)
spl:
`notable` urgency IN ("high","critical") earliest=-14d
| eval awareness_time=strptime(status_description,"(?i)awareness[:\\s]+(%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S)")
| eval early_warning_sent=strptime(status_description,"(?i)early.warning[:\\s]+(%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S)")
| eval notification_72h=strptime(status_description,"(?i)72h.notification[:\\s]+(%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S)")
| eval hours_to_early=round((early_warning_sent-awareness_time)/3600,2)
| eval hours_to_72=round((notification_72h-awareness_time)/3600,2)
| where hours_to_early > 24 OR hours_to_72 > 72 OR isnull(early_warning_sent)
| table rule_name, awareness_time, early_warning_sent, notification_72h, hours_to_early, hours_to_72
description: National transpositions specify early warning and incident notification deadlines. This use case extends UC-22.2.1 by explicitly clocking awareness time, regulatory filing timestamps, and CSIRT handoff fields — separating internal detection latency from legal notification compliance.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIS2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Train analysts to record structured timestamps in `status_description` or use custom fields via adaptive response; (2) map incident classes to which deadlines apply per national law; (3) alert legal at T-2h before each deadline; (4) retain immutable export of closed incidents; (5) integrate with UC-22.2.19 for cross-border cases. Indexes required: `index=itsm`.
visualization: Timeline (milestones per incident), Table (deadline breaches), Single value (mean hours to early warning). Key fields: `awareness_time`, `early_warning_sent`, `hours_to_72`, `hours_to_early`, `notification_72h`, `rule_name`.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `` `notable` `` macro, `index=itsm` (regulatory filing tasks with custom fields).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Train analysts to record structured timestamps in `status_description` or use custom fields via adaptive response; (2) map incident classes to which deadlines apply per national law; (3) alert legal at T-2h before each deadline; (4) retain immutable export of closed incidents; (5) integrate with UC-22.2.19 for cross-border cases.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
`notable` urgency IN ("high","critical") earliest=-14d
| eval awareness_time=strptime(status_description,"(?i)awareness[:\\s]+(%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S)")
| eval early_warning_sent=strptime(status_description,"(?i)early.warning[:\\s]+(%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S)")
| eval notification_72h=strptime(status_description,"(?i)72h.notification[:\\s]+(%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S)")
| eval hours_to_early=round((early_warning_sent-awareness_time)/3600,2)
| eval hours_to_72=round((notification_72h-awareness_time)/3600,2)
| where hours_to_early > 24 OR hours_to_72 > 72 OR isnull(early_warning_sent)
| table rule_name, awareness_time, early_warning_sent, notification_72h, hours_to_early, hours_to_72
```

Understanding this SPL

**NIS2 Incident Reporting Timeline Compliance (24h Early Warning / 72h Notification) (Art. 23)** — National transpositions specify early warning and incident notification deadlines. This use case extends UC-22.2.1 by explicitly clocking awareness time, regulatory filing timestamps, and CSIRT handoff fields — separating internal detection latency from legal notification compliance.

Documented **Data sources**: `` `notable` `` macro, `index=itsm` (regulatory filing tasks with custom fields). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Invokes macro `notable` — in Search, use the UI or expand to inspect the underlying SPL.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **awareness_time** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **early_warning_sent** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **notification_72h** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **hours_to_early** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **hours_to_72** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where hours_to_early > 24 OR hours_to_72 > 72 OR isnull(early_warning_sent)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: awareness_time, early_warning_sent, hours_to_72, hours_to_early, notification_72h, rule_name.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (milestones per incident), Table (deadline breaches), Single value (mean hours to early warning).
grandmaExplanation: We watch for national transpositions specify early warning and incident notification deadlines. this use case extends .2.1 by explicitly clocking awareness time, regulatory filing timestamps, and csirt han. This is critical because gaps here could mean serious regulatory exposure or security blind spots.
